generated_oml:
// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor capturing temperature data for contextual data trace aggregation in manufacturing environments, aligned with system requirements for real-time monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance PressureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor measuring pressure parameters for operational metrics collection, integrated into distributed sensing infrastructure."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PressureDataTransmitted
]

instance VibrationSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Physical sensor detecting vibration patterns to support predictive maintenance and performance analysis in industrial systems."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VibrationDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance TemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance PressureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PressureSensor
]

instance VibrationDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VibrationSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance ContextualDataTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from physical sensors, structured as time-series or event-based datasets for analysis and abstraction."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmitted, PressureDataTransmitted, VibrationDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLModelAggregator, DataPreprocessingEngine
]

instance FunctionalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML-based functional architecture model representing system requirements and functional decomposition for validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLSimulationEngine
]

instance LogicalArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "MontiArc or AADL-based logical architecture model defining component-connector systems with behavioral specifications."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MontiArcSimulationEngine
]

instance BehavioralStateMachineModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML state machine or MontiArc Automaton model describing system behavior for simulation and validation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkBehavioralSimulator
]

instance SimulationEnvironmentModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation model integrated with Simulink or other simulation engines for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLModelAggregator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MBSE tool aggregating SysML models (IBDs, requirements) for cross-layer validation and traceability."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalAnalysisService, RequirementsVerificationService
]

instance DataPreprocessingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Engine performing normalization, filtering, and anomaly detection on sensor data before aggregation."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataQualityService
]

instance SysMLSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML-compatible simulation engine validating functional and logical architecture models against requirements."
    DTDFVocab:enables FunctionalAnalysisService
]

instance MontiArcSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MontiArc-based simulation platform executing logical architecture models with behavioral specifications."
    DTDFVocab:enables LogicalValidationService
]

instance SimulinkBehavioralSimulator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink platform for detailed behavioral modeling and dynamic system simulation."
    DTDFVocab:enables BehavioralSimulationService
]

instance SimulinkSimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink environment for physics-based simulation and performance analysis of system models."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicsSimulationService
]

instance ModelIntegrationPlatform : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Platform unifying heterogeneous models (SysML, ADLs, simulation outputs) into cohesive Digital Twin representation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelConsistencyService
]

// C6: Services
instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service aggregating and abstracting contextual data traces for real-time or historical analysis of physical system state."
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeMonitoringInsight, HistoricalAnalysisInsight
]

instance OptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service leveraging model-based predictions to suggest improvements in system performance, resource allocation, or operational efficiency."
    DTDFVocab:provides PerformanceOptimizationInsight, ResourceAllocationInsight
]

instance PredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service using behavioral models to forecast system behavior, failures, or performance degradation."
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionInsight, PerformanceDegradationInsight
]

instance ControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Active service acting as intermediary to push data or commands back to physical system for autonomous decision-making."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlCommandAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing multi-abstraction views (functional, logical, physical) using SysML IBDs or other MBSE tools."
    DTDFVocab:provides FunctionalViewInsight, LogicalViewInsight, PhysicalViewInsight
]

instance FunctionalAnalysisService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing SysML functional analysis to validate system design against requirements."
    DTDFVocab:provides DesignValidationInsight
]

instance RequirementsVerificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service verifying system requirements through model-based validation processes."
    DTDFVocab:provides RequirementsComplianceInsight
]

instance LogicalValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating logical architecture models against functional requirements."
    DTDFVocab:provides LogicalArchitectureValidationInsight
]

instance BehavioralSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service executing behavioral simulations to validate system dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehavioralValidationInsight
]

instance PhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service performing physics-based simulations for performance analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicsValidationInsight
]

instance DataQualityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring data accuracy through preprocessing and validation."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataAccuracyInsight
]

instance ModelConsistencyService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service maintaining consistency across heterogeneous models in the Digital Twin."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelConsistencyInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system described in the provided documents emphasizes **temporal synchronization** through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, which often involve **multi-level abstraction** (e.g., requirements, functional, logical, and physical layers) to manage complexity. While explicit synchronization frequencies or latency requirements are not detailed, the integration of **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **SysML IBDs**, **AADL**, or **MontiArc**—combined with **behavioral modeling tools** (e.g., **SysML state machines**, **MathWorks Simulink**, or **general-purpose simulation tools**)—implies **real-time or near-real-time data flows** for active Digital Twin components. The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** (Kritzinger et al., [32]) suggests **one-way data aggregation** with **contextual granularity** (e.g., traces, abstraction layers) but does not specify temporal granularity. For Digital Twins, the system may require **latency-sensitive coordination** (e.g., <100ms for control loops) when integrating with **active components** (e.g., interpreters or processors) to enable bidirectional data flow, though exact time scales are not quantified in the documents."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **Digital Twin constellation** is orchestrated via **MBSE frameworks** integrating **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., **SysML**, **Simulink**, **C++/Java**) and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **MontiArc Automaton** or **SysML state machines** for behavior modeling. **Component integration** relies on **component-connector paradigms** (e.g., **ACME**, **AADL**), where **interfaces**, **interconnections**, and **composition** define system structure. **Resource management** is implied through **layered abstraction** (e.g., logical twins managing computational resources for physical twins), but **system-wide coordination** lacks explicit protocols (e.g., **DDS**, **MQTT**, or **gRPC**). The **Digital Shadow Reference Model** ([32]) suggests **passive aggregation** of data traces, while Digital Twins may include **active components** (e.g., interpreters) for **bidirectional data flow**, though **orchestration patterns** (e.g., **microservices**, **event-driven architectures**) are not specified."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) system is structured through **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** methodologies, encompassing multiple abstraction layers: **requirements, functional architecture, logical architecture, and product/technical layers**. The transition between these stages is governed by methodologies such as **RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics)**, **OOSEM (Object-Oriented Systems Engineering Methodology)**, **SPES (Systematic Process for Engineering Systems)**, and **SMArDT (Smart Digital Twin Methodology)**. Each layer employs distinct **architecture description languages (ADLs)** like **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML Internal Block Diagrams (IBDs)** to model components, interfaces, connectors, and composition. Behavior modeling is further refined using **state machines in SysML v1.7** or **MontiArc Automaton**, while detailed simulations are integrated via tools such as **MathWorks Simulink, general-purpose simulation methods, or code in C++/Java**. Lifecycle governance involves **heterogeneous stakeholder integration** and **multi-tool interoperability**, ensuring seamless transitions between modeling notations and tools across phases. The lifecycle is iterative, with continuous refinement from abstract requirements to executable technical models, adhering to structured **MBSE frameworks** for consistency and traceability.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system enforces **model fidelity requirements** through multi-layered validation and verification (V&V) methodologies, including:
- **Model fidelity**: Achieved via **abstraction hierarchies** (e.g., RFLP [18], OOSEM [20]) to ensure alignment between requirements, functional/logical architectures, and physical layers. **Architecture description languages (ADLs)** like AADL or MontiArc enforce structural fidelity by defining component interfaces, connectors, and composition rules.
- **Validation methods**: Combination of **formal verification** (e.g., state machine analysis in SysML v1.7) and **simulation-based testing** (e.g., MathWorks Simulink for behavioral dynamics). Uncertainty quantification is implied via **data aggregation and abstraction** (as per [32]) to reconcile real-time sensor data with model predictions.
- **Uncertainty sources**: Inherent in **sensor noise**, model parameter drift, and environmental variability. Quantification relies on **contextual data traces** (e.g., manufacturing analytics) to identify deviations.
- **Error detection/correction**: Active components of the Digital Twin (e.g., [35]–[37]) may employ **closed-loop mechanisms** (e.g., feedback from simulation tools) to correct discrepancies, though explicit protocols are not detailed.
- **Quality assurance processes**: Enforced via **MBSE methodologies** (e.g., SysML, ADLs) to ensure traceability across abstraction layers. **Tool integration** (e.g., Simulink + SysML) supports cross-verification of models and data.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The technical network infrastructure for Digital Twin systems is designed to support **low-latency, high-reliability communication** between physical and digital components, incorporating the following specifications:
  - **Communication Protocols**: Primarily **OPC UA** (for industrial interoperability) and **MQTT** (for lightweight, publish-subscribe architectures), with support for **TCP/IP** or **UDP** as transport layers. For complex systems, **ROS (Robot Operating System)** or **DDS (Data Distribution Service)** may be employed to enable real-time data exchange.
  - **Network Topology and Architecture**: Typically follows a **hybrid topology** combining **cloud-edge-on-premise** deployments, with **mesh or star architectures** for distributed systems. Critical paths may use **dedicated low-latency networks** (e.g., Ethernet TSN or 5G private networks) to minimize latency.
  - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Latency constraints are system-dependent (e.g., **<10ms for control loops**, **<1s for monitoring**), with bandwidth demands scaling with data volume (e.g., **100s of Mbps for high-resolution sensor streams**). **Quality of Service (QoS) policies** (e.g., in DDS or MQTT) prioritize critical data flows.
  - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Implements **failover protocols** (e.g., **active-active clustering**), **data replication**, and **checksum validation** to ensure continuity. For industrial applications, **ISO 26262-compliant** redundancy may be mandated for safety-critical systems.
  - **Security Protocols**: Enforces **TLS/SSL encryption**, **authentication** (e.g., OAuth 2.0, X.509 certificates), and **access control** (e.g., role-based policies) to protect data integrity. **Zero-trust architectures** and **network segmentation** are recommended for high-security environments."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The deployment infrastructure for Digital Twin systems is optimized for **scalability, performance, and resilience**, leveraging the following technical configurations:
  - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **multi-cloud (AWS, Azure, Google Cloud)**, **edge computing** (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Raspberry Pi clusters), and **on-premise** deployments (e.g., private data centers). **Hybrid cloud-edge architectures** are common for latency-sensitive applications.
  - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: Computational demands vary by use case—**GPU-accelerated workloads** (e.g., for physics simulations in SysML or Simulink) may require **NVIDIA Tesla or AMD Instinct GPUs**, while storage scales from **petabytes for historical data** (e.g., time-series databases like InfluxDB) to **low-latency SSDs for real-time processing**.
  - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Employs **containerization** (e.g., Docker, Kubernetes) for dynamic resource allocation, **auto-scaling policies** (e.g., based on CPU/memory thresholds), and **sharding** for distributed databases. **Serverless architectures** (e.g., AWS Lambda) may be used for sporadic workloads.
  - **Deployment Models Supported**: Includes **monolithic deployments** (for legacy systems), **microservices** (for modular Digital Twin components), and **event-driven architectures** (e.g., using Apache Kafka or AWS EventBridge). **CI/CD pipelines** (e.g., Jenkins, GitLab CI) ensure rapid, automated deployments.
  - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Achieved through **multi-region replication**, **disaster recovery plans** (e.g., RTO < 15 mins, RPO < 1 hour), and **high-availability (HA) clusters**. **SLAs** (e.g., 99.99% uptime) are enforced via **monitoring tools** (e.g., Prometheus, Grafana) and **automated failover mechanisms**."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is facilitated through **interoperability standards** and **heterogeneous data exchange protocols** implied by the **MBSE ecosystem**. The system integrates **architecture description languages (ADLs)** (e.g., **SysML**, **AADL**) with **simulation tools** (e.g., **Simulink**, **general-purpose languages like C++/Java**) and **external enterprise systems** via **model-based interfaces**. **Dependencies on external systems** (e.g., IoT platforms, ERP systems) are not detailed, but the **Digital Shadow Reference Model** ([32]) highlights **one-way data flows** for contextual aggregation, suggesting **APIs** or **message brokers** (e.g., **REST**, **SOAP**) for integration. **Interoperability standards** may include **OPC UA**, **ROS**, or **industry-specific protocols** (e.g., **MODBUS**), though explicit mention is absent. The system does not explicitly describe **federated Digital Twin networks** or **cross-organizational integration patterns**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system for the MABI MAX 100 serial robot incorporates multiple **safety-critical and cybersecurity measures** to ensure operational integrity and threat mitigation. The **safety cell** restricts the maximum working range of the first axis to **[-90°, 90°]** and enforces a **maximum speed of |q̇_max| = 75°/s**, preventing unsafe movements. The system employs **dual position encoders** (motor encoder and secondary encoder behind the gearbox) to reduce backlash and positional uncertainty, enabling real-time monitoring of drive train elasticity and friction.

The robot is controlled via the **Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl. CNC controller**, which enforces **fail-safe mechanisms** through strict input/output validation (e.g., position, speed, acceleration) and **authenticated access control** for operational commands. **Torque and current monitoring** (τ_M, I_M) are integrated into the control loop, allowing for **real-time torque feedback** and anomaly detection (e.g., overload or mechanical failure). The system also supports **positional accuracy validation** (±0.1 mm repeatability) and **positional uncertainty reduction** via encoder differential analysis.

For **cybersecurity**, the CNC controller likely implements **hardware-level authentication** (e.g., Siemens’ proprietary security protocols) and **role-based access control** to restrict unauthorized modifications to control parameters. While explicit **risk assessment methodologies** (e.g., ISO 27001, IEC 62443) or **data anonymization techniques** are not detailed, the **closed-loop control architecture** inherently limits exposure of sensitive operational data (e.g., motor current, torque) to external systems unless explicitly configured for telemetry. The **digital shadow** of the first axis (driven by a permanent magnet synchronous motor) may require **secure data transmission protocols** (e.g., encrypted communication channels) if integrated into a broader IoT/Digital Twin ecosystem, though this is not explicitly mentioned in the provided context."
]
